description_long: This object is a classic cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a bulky, deep chassis and a slightly curved glass screen. The front features a wide, black plastic bezel surrounding the display, with a row of control buttons and input ports located along the bottom edge. The sides and rear are composed of thick, molded black plastic, forming a pronounced taper that accommodates the CRT's depth. Ventilation grilles are visible on the upper rear section, allowing heat dissipation. The screen displays static, indicating it is powered on but not receiving a signal. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of late 20th-century consumer electronics.
description: A bulky, black CRT television with a curved glass screen, thick plastic casing, and front-facing control buttons and ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved glass screen, static display, thick black bezel, and a row of control buttons and ports at the bottom.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep, tapered plastic housing and the pronounced rear bulge typical of CRT televisions.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the wide, angular back with ventilation grilles and the overall depth of the television.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, emphasizing the thick, molded plastic and the CRT's extended rear section.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, black plastic bezel with a matte finish and utilitarian styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: row of buttons on the bottom edge
